channel.title: Wellness on PepsFeeder
channel.description: Recent content in Wellness on PepsFeeder

item.title: Brain Health, Mood, and Cognitive Disorders: What Modern Science Actually Shows
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: How Heat Affects the Brain The human brain is a complex organ that is sensitive to a variety of factors, including temperature. Heat can have both positive and negative effects on the brain, depending on the specific circumstances.

item.title: Food Safety and Storage: Risks Hidden in Everyday Choices
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: How Long Does Rice Last in the Fridge? And Other Pressing Rice Questions, Answered Rice is a staple food in many cultures, and it’s easy to see why.

item.title: Addiction, Pregnancy, and Public Health: Biological and Social Realities
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Pregnant, Addicted and Fighting the Pull of Drugs By JENNIFER SCHNEIDER The Associated Press CINCINNATI – The baby’s father was a drug dealer. The mother was a drug addict.

item.title: Kind Thoughts Strategy: How to Become Tolerant of the Unknown
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: In March, I asked on social networks what was the most difficult for people during the COVID-19 pandemic. I got more than 100 answers and found that in many ways people have the same problem: the unknown.

item.title: DIY - 24 method how to make your own hand sanitizer
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Liquid hand sanitizers-mostly alcohol-based gels-have experienced an explosion in popularity over the last 10 years. If you’ve recently traveled by plane or set foot in a classroom in the US, chances are you’ve seen hand sanitizers in use.

item.title: Don't rush to deploy coronavirus vaccines and drugs
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Around the world, I am seeing efforts to support ‘quick-fix’ programmes aimed at developing vaccines and therapeutics against COVID-19. Groups in the United States and China are already planning to test vaccines in healthy human volunteers.

item.title: Gut Health, Constipation, and the Truth About “Digestive Boosters”
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Are Gut Boosters BS? Supplement companies claim that prebiotics (which are one thing) and probiotics (another thing!) can strengthen immunity, aid weight loss, and even extend life.

item.title: Medical mind-reading brain scanners to ask patients who cannot speak
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Doctors are using brain scanners to ask patients who cannot speak about their treatment wishes When a person sustains a severe brain injury that leaves them unable to communicate, their families and doctors often have to make life-or-death decisions about their care for them.

item.title: The healthiest and easiest Mediterranean diet to follow
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Discover the benefits and keys of one of the most ideal diets for our health and figure. With weekly downloadable menu included. Every day you get up with new diets, more blacklisted names of banned products and new superfoods.

item.title: Sleep, Tea, and Lifestyle Interventions: Non-Pharmacological Approaches to Rest and Recovery
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: 4 types of tea to help you sleep To sleep better, we may need to drink some beneficial teas. In her report, published by the American “Basel” website, author Elizabeth Inks reviewed the types of tea that have relaxing properties, which help them sleep faster and for a longer period of time.

item.title: Infectious Disease Breakthroughs and Media Hype
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Doctors in Thailand announce discovery of the deadly corona virus treatment Doctors in Thailand said they had successfully treated one of the patients with the Coronavir virus with a combination of antiviral drugs, according to a briefing yesterday from the Ministry of Health reported by CNN.

item.title: Cancer Warning Signs, Risk Factors, and Early Detection: Separating Hype from Evidence
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: When does throat pain indicate a thyroid tumor The German Association of Otolaryngologists said that sore throat is not due to a common cold, especially if it is not accompanied by the distinct symptoms of a cold, which are runny nose, sneezing, and coughing.

item.title: Men’s Reproductive and Hormonal Health: Warning Signs You Shouldn’t Ignore
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: What are the causes of testicle pain Most men are concerned when they experience pain in the testicles, but not all pain is an indication of a serious illness.

item.title: Intermittent fasting advantages and the right method for you
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Intermittent fasting or part-time fasting is not only the ideal introduction to ketosis, but it also has several advantages for your performance and performance. You can find out about the effects of skipping a meal every now and then and which method you can use to start intermittent fasting.

item.title: The discovery leads to drugs targeting dozens of types of cancer
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: By turning off genes one by one, researchers from the United Kingdom and the United States have targeted cancer with new drugs. The author, Sean Lintern, said in a report in the British newspaper “The Independent”, that scientists have made an important step towards developing new drugs that target specific genes within dozens of types of cancer.

item.title: Stimulant alternatives that keep you away from coffee
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: People often drink coffee when they feel lethargic or when they are drowsy and want to get rid of it. However, there are six food substitutes for coffee that give the body a lot of energy and energy.

item.title: Magic fruits that help you get rid of belly
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: The German Nutrition Association reported that there are types of fruits that contain important nutrients that help remove belly fat (rumen), which negatively affects the body, and increase the risk of diabetes, heart disease, and metabolism:

item.title: Natural Remedies, Herbal Claims, and Immune Support: What Works and What Doesn’t
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: 4 weapons to fight viruses The German magazine “Freuden” provided some advice and instructions that can protect against viruses and strengthen the immune system to fight viruses in the body, namely:

item.title: The world is heading towards vegetarianism and these are its advantages and dangers
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: (AZ) An Egyptian engineer, thirty, tall, with a sporty appearance, with a sarcastic smile mixed with confusion. He says, “After our marriage, the bride prepared a table full of barbecues, but she went crazy when she left making her hands looking for a boiled egg and a piece of cheese, so I wrote on Facebook.

item.title: Do you enjoy an iron memory or suffer from the scourge of forgetfulness?
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Turning the house upside down in search of car keys just two minutes before leaving the house is a classic. Sometimes you try to focus, remember and review every second you passed the day before, and you certainly tried to remember the name of the hero of the movie you are watching with your friend, but to no avail.

item.title: Metabolic Health After 30: Belly Fat, Diabetes, and the Hidden Role of Processed Foods
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: 7 foods useful for the health of the pancreas The pancreas plays a key role in the digestion and regulation of blood sugar levels. The pancreas produces two types of secretions: the first is excreted to the gut, where it secretes a juicer containing digestive enzymes.

item.title: 9 Successful Steps to Quit Smoking Electronic Cigarettes
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: You may have heard that e-cigarette smoking causes fatal lung disease for its users. If you are ready to quit, these strategies will help. In her report published by The Healthy website, author Andrea Barbalic said that the dangers of smoking e-cigarettes are becoming more pronounced for medical experts and the general public.

item.title: How to read the results of the blood test?
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: A blood test is usually performed as part of a comprehensive medical examination or prior to surgical interventions. However, this analysis includes several vague abbreviations such as LDL, GPT or RBC, whose meaning is difficult to interpret.

item.title: What are coffee diseases?
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Coffee is a popular drink, and some people say they can’t imagine living without it, but drinking too much can lead to “caffeine poisoning”. Dr. Walid Sarhan, a Jordanian psychiatrist, says that coffee is a favorite international beverage, which is surpassed only in oil by international trade.

item.title: 9 dangerous foods people continue to eat
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: There are dangerous foods that people eat and may lead to death, not only a hamburger provided by some fast-food restaurants or French fries and all products that are high in saturated fat, but there are many other types.

item.title: Sleep disorders .. What causes them? Where are the risks?
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Some people suffer from sleep disorders, what causes them? What are the risks? How can they be confronted? Dr. Ulrich Fodderholzer says sleep disorders mean an inability to enjoy a restful and comfortable sleep at least three nights a week for more than four weeks.

item.title: Sexually transmitted infections
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Many patients believe that since they’ve been with the same partner for many years, they can’t get a sexually transmitted infection and therefore they don’t need to get tested.

item.title: The ultimate joints pain guide from zero to finish
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: One of the most health issues that confront the 21th-century population is joint pain from knee pain to neck pain, ankle, hip or pelvic pain, especially for people who suffer from severe health problems.

item.title: 46 health myths most people believe in
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: We all heard at some stage in our lives some advice from our parents, family or friends that we looked after to appear that it is totally false, maybe even the opposite of truth, people to transfer a lot of talk mouth to mouth without checking after it, we tried in this article to compile a list of the most common myths people believe in.

item.title: Nutrients that may change your health forever and for good
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: So we all know that the food that we eat contains calories but looking into it more we could consider calories as energy, we can split the energy into free main types known as macronutrients these free are known as protein carbohydrates and fat.

item.title: Anorexia - You will not believe how this eating disorder can transform people
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Patients with anorexia nervosa the most lethal psychiatric condition feel compelled to diet and exercise despite negative consequences restricting food become automatic and consuming high-calorie foods causes anxiety.

item.title: Nutrient Deficiencies and What Your Body Reveals
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: 7 Signs That You May Be Suffering From Vitamin D Deficiency According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), 32 percent of adults and children in the U.

item.title: Woman Wears Mascara To Bed For 25 Years And Never Washes Her Face - See Under Her Eyelid
item.author: support@pepsfeeder.com (Chris Mano)
item.description: Most of us wear our makeup to bed on occasion. We may come in and just be too tired to wash it off so we just go to bed.
